role: Health System Transformation Research Project Coordinator
location: Portland, OR (Hybrid / primarily remote with required in-Oregon travel)
apply_url_note: Exact iCIMS req not resolvable via search (JS-rendered portal). Verified-live source + apply route is the LinkedIn posting; human-finish lane should reach the iCIMS req via LinkedIn 'Apply' or by keyword-searching 'Research Project Coordinator' in the OHSU external iCIMS portal. (Old req 32001 'Research Project Coordinator, Entry' returns 410 Gone — different/expired posting, not this one.)
jd_summary: ORPRN (Oregon Rural Practice-based Research Network) coordinator for health systems transformation initiatives. Manages timelines, deliverables, and team meetings; builds relationships with funders, providers, payers, government partners, and community-based organizations; drafts proposals/manuscripts/reports; develops and implements study protocols and leads junior staff; recruits participants and conducts qualitative/quantitative data analysis; trains staff in quality improvement and systems change. Primarily remote with required travel around Oregon.
yoe_required: Master's degree in relevant field + 1 year experience (or equivalent). Public health competencies, implementation science / QI / systems change, advanced MS Office. Preferred: MPH, project coordination experience, bilingual EN/ES.
sponsorship_note: OHSU is a cap-exempt employer (sidesteps H-1B lottery; sponsorship feasible). Posting contains NO explicit 'no sponsorship', ITAR, or US-Person clause. No hard stop.
